Information about the file to update along with the new properties to set. The /files/update API accepts requests in the following format:

UpdateFileNameRequest Model - Request to update the name of a file.
projectId integer
required
example: 23456789
Id of the project that contains the file.
name string
required
example: file1.py
The current name of the file.
newName string
required
example: file2.py
The new name for the file.
codeSourceId string
example: MCP Server
Name of the environment that's creating the request.
Example 
{
  "projectId": 23456789,
  "name": "file1.py",
  "newName": "file2.py",
  "codeSourceId": "MCP Server"
}
UpdateFileContentsRequest Model - Request to update the contents of a file.
projectId integer
required
example: 23456789
Id of the project that contains the file.
name string
required
example: main.py
The name of the file to update.
content string
required
example: class CustomClass: def __init__(self): pass
The new contents of the file.
codeSourceId string
example: MCP Server
Name of the environment that's creating the request.
Example 
{
  "projectId": 23456789,
  "name": "main.py",
  "content": "class CustomClass:
    def __init__(self):
        pass
",
  "codeSourceId": "MCP Server"
}
